items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: Nuclear option should get serious consideration from NBA to combat tanking
items.blurb: Nuclear option should get serious consideration from NBA to combat tanking — Try as the NBA might, but tanking isn't going away. While pondering how to de-incentivize losing, commissioner Adam Silver must consider the nuclear option.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Suns 134, Jazz 109
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Suns 134, Jazz 109 — Jalen Green scored a game-high 31 points, Devin Booker dropped 26 points while adding eight assists, and Grayson Allen picked up 19 points off the bench as the Phoenix Suns defeated the Utah Jazz 134-109.

items.headline: Paul George plays hero for 76ers in second game after suspension
items.blurb: Paul George plays hero for 76ers in second game after suspension — In only his second game since serving a 25-game suspension for violating the NBA's anti-drug policy, Paul George played the role of hero for the Philadelphia 76ers in their win over the Charlotte Hornets on Saturday night.

items.headline: NBA Roundup: Pistons strengthen hold on East’s top seed
items.blurb: NBA Roundup: Pistons strengthen hold on East’s top seed — Tobias Harris led a balanced scoring attack with 18 points on Saturday as the Detroit Pistons strengthened their hold on the top seed in the Eastern Conference with a 109-87 victory over the injury-riddled Minnesota Timberwolves.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Grizzlies 125, Bulls 124
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Grizzlies 125, Bulls 124 — Cedric Coward led the Memphis Grizzlies with 24 points and Tyler Burton added 18 points off the bench as the Grizzlies held on for a 125-124 win over the Chicago Bulls.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Hawks 123, Kings 113
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Hawks 123, Kings 113 — Nickeil Alexander-Walker led the way with 27 points and Jalen Johnson added 26 points and 10 assists as the Atlanta Hawks defeated the Sacramento Kings 123-113.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: 76ers 118, Hornets 114
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: 76ers 118, Hornets 114 — Joel Embiid had a team-high 29 points and Paul George added a 26-point, 13-rebound double-double to help the Philadelphia 76ers beat the Charlotte Hornets 118-114.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Pistons 109, Timberwolves 87
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Pistons 109, Timberwolves 87 — Tobias Harris had 18 points and Jalen Duran had 10 points and 13 rebounds as the Detroit Pistons topped the Minnesota Timberwolves 109-87.

items.headline: Doc Rivers Said Health Issues, Lack Of Depth, Giannis Antetokounmpo Rumors Undid Bucks Season
items.blurb: Doc Rivers Said Health Issues, Lack Of Depth, Giannis Antetokounmpo Rumors Undid Bucks Season — The Milwaukee Bucks were officially eliminated from postseason contention after their home loss to the San Antonio Spurs on Saturday. items.headline: NBA suspends Lakers’ Doncic one game after 16th technical foul of season
items.blurb: NBA suspends Lakers’ Doncic one game after 16th technical foul of season — The Los Angeles Lakers superstar has been suspended one game by the NBA after receiving his 16th technical foul of the regular season.

items.headline: 76ers’ Tyrese Maxey returns from 10-game absence vs. Hornets
items.blurb: 76ers’ Tyrese Maxey returns from 10-game absence vs. Hornets — All-star guard Tyrese Maxey is returning to the 76ers lineup after missing 10 consecutive games due to a finger injury, the team announced on Saturday.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Spurs 127, Bucks 95
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Spurs 127, Bucks 95 — Stephon Castle put up a 22-10-10 triple-double, Victor Wembanyama had 23 points and 15 rebounds on the night, and the San Antonio Spurs took a 127-95 win over the Milwaukee Bucks.

items.headline: Adama Bal, Grizzlies Sign 10 Day Contract
items.blurb: Adama Bal, Grizzlies Sign 10 Day Contract — The Memphis Grizzlies have signed forward Adama Bal to a hardship 10 Day contract. Memphis ruled several players out for the season due to injuries. In addition, the Grizzlies have other players dealing with various non-season-ending injuries. Bal has spent the season with the Westchester Knicks of the G League after going undrafted out of Santa Clara at

items.headline: Doc Rivers Unsure If Kevin Porter Jr Will Play Again This Season
items.blurb: Doc Rivers Unsure If Kevin Porter Jr Will Play Again This Season — Milwaukee Bucks guard Kevin Porter Jr's season may be coming to an early end. Porter is dealing with synovitis in his right knee. Bucks head coach Doc Rivers said he'd be "surprised" if Porter plays again this season. items.headline: Stephen Curry 'Running Out Of Games' To Return This Season
items.blurb: Stephen Curry 'Running Out Of Games' To Return This Season — Steve Kerr said Friday the Golden State Warriors are running out of games to facilitate Stephen Curry's return from a knee injury, marking the first time the organization has publicly acknowledged the window may be closing on the star guard's season. items.headline: Josh Giddey: Triple-doubles in defeat
items.blurb: Josh Giddey: Triple-doubles in defeat — Giddey recorded 18 points (6-11 FG, 1-3 3Pt, 5-5 FT), 13 rebounds, 10 assists, three blocks and two steals across 35 minutes in Saturday's 125-124 loss to the Grizzlies. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Sterling Brown Wins First EuroLeague MVP Of The Round Award After 31-Point Game
items.blurb: Sterling Brown Wins First EuroLeague MVP Of The Round Award After 31-Point Game — Sterling Brown earned his first EuroLeague MVP of the Round distinction in Round 34, delivering 31 points and a PIR of 32 as Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade defeated Valencia Basket 110-104 in double overtime at Belgrade Arena. Brown shot 5-of-10 on two-pointers, 4-of-9 from three-point range and a perfect 9-of-9 from

items.headline: Luka Doncic Faces One-Game Suspension After 16th Technical Foul In Lakers' Win Over Nets
items.blurb: Luka Doncic Faces One-Game Suspension After 16th Technical Foul In Lakers' Win Over Nets — Luka Doncic reached the NBA's suspension threshold Friday, drawing his 16th technical foul of the season during the Los Angeles Lakers' 116-99 victory over the Brooklyn Nets. Barring a successful appeal, Doncic will miss Monday's home game against the Washington Wizards. The technical was assessed with 5:12

items.headline: Caleb Wilson Leaves North Carolina To Declare For 2026 NBA Draft
items.blurb: Caleb Wilson Leaves North Carolina To Declare For 2026 NBA Draft — Caleb Wilson, a projected high lottery selection who missed the final nine games of North Carolina's season with hand and thumb injuries, has declared for the 2026 NBA Draft. The 6-foot-10, 215-pound forward announced his decision via Instagram, captioning a photo carousel with "never take it for granted." Wilson averaged 19.8 poin

items.headline: Yaxel Lendeborg Posts 23/12/7 Line As Michigan Beats Alabama 90-77 To Reach Elite Eight
items.blurb: Yaxel Lendeborg Posts 23/12/7 Line As Michigan Beats Alabama 90-77 To Reach Elite Eight — Yaxel Lendeborg turned in one of the tournament's most complete individual performances Friday, powering top-seeded Michigan past fourth-seeded Alabama 90-77 in the Sweet 16. The 6-foot-9 forward recorded 23 points, 12 rebounds and seven assists at the United Center in Chicago. Michigan trailed by nine early

items.headline: Tennessee Reaches Third Straight Elite Eight, Will Face Michigan After Routing Iowa State 76-62
items.blurb: Tennessee Reaches Third Straight Elite Eight, Will Face Michigan After Routing Iowa State 76-62 — The sixth-seeded Tennessee Volunteers dismantled Iowa State 76-62 at the United Center in Chicago, setting up a Midwest Region final Sunday against top-seeded Michigan. Eleven of Tennessee's players are newcomers this season. Coach Rick Barnes assembled the group with clear ambitions from the start. "

items.headline: Tarris Reed Jr. Delivers At Free Throw Line As UConn Survives Michigan State In Sweet 16
items.blurb: Tarris Reed Jr. Delivers At Free Throw Line As UConn Survives Michigan State In Sweet 16 — Tarris Reed Jr. overcame a known weakness Friday night, converting all four of his late free throws to help second-seeded UConn defeat third-seeded Michigan State 67-63 in the Sweet 16 at Capital One Arena in Washington. Reed, shooting just 59% from the line this season, sank two decisive foul shots with 44.

items.headline: Duke Beats St. John's To Advance To Elite Eight As Caleb Foster Delivers Clutch Injury Return
items.blurb: Duke Beats St. John's To Advance To Elite Eight As Caleb Foster Delivers Clutch Injury Return — Twenty days after fracturing his right foot and 19 days after surgery, Duke guard Caleb Foster came off the bench to score 11 second-half points, lifting the No. 1 overall seed Blue Devils past St. John's 80-75 Friday at Capital One Arena. The victory sends Duke to its third consecutive Elite Eight wher

items.headline: Former Players Currently In NBA Of Elite Eight Schools In 2026 NCAA Tournament
items.blurb: Former Players Currently In NBA Of Elite Eight Schools In 2026 NCAA Tournament — Duke has the most former alumni in the NBA out of the Elite 8 schools with 27 players. Arizona and Michigan each have 10 or more players currently in the NBA. Here's how the eight schools in the 2026 NCAA Tournament are represented: East Region (Washington, D.C.) 1. Duke (27) 2. UConn (9) West Region (San Jose, CA) 1.

items.headline: Jalen Johnson: Another double-double
items.blurb: Jalen Johnson: Another double-double — Johnson posted 26 points (11-22 FG, 4-1 3Pt), 10 assists, five rebounds and one steal over 37 minutes during the Hawks' 123-113 win over the Kings on Saturday. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Nickeil Alexander-Walker: Strong performance Saturday
items.blurb: Nickeil Alexander-Walker: Strong performance Saturday — Alexander-Walker logged 27 points (7-14 FG, 4-9 3Pt, 9-9 FT), eight assists, five rebounds and four steals over 36 minutes during the Hawks' 123-113 win over the Kings on Saturday. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Paul George: Full stat line in win
items.blurb: Paul George: Full stat line in win — George racked up 26 points (9-19 FG, 4-11 3Pt, 4-5 FT), 13 rebounds, four steals, two assists and one block across 36 minutes in Saturday's 118-114 win over the Hornets. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Tyrese Maxey: No rust in return
items.blurb: Tyrese Maxey: No rust in return — Maxey (finger) generated 26 points (10-18 FG, 3-6 3Pt, 3-4 FT), eight assists, seven rebounds, one steal and one block across 43 minutes in Saturday's 118-114 win over the Hornets. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Sun Sold To Fertitta Family, Will Move To Houston In 2027
items.blurb: Sun Sold To Fertitta Family, Will Move To Houston In 2027 — The Connecticut Sun are being sold to the Fertitta family and will relocate to Houston, sources told Alexa Philippou of ESPN. The Fertitta family are purchasing the team for $300 million, a record-breaking figure for a WNBA side. That figure also includes a relocation fee. The Sun will play one more season in Uncasville, Connecticut befor

items.headline: Tempo Win Coin Toss, Elect To Pick Sixth In First Round Of WNBA Draft
items.blurb: Tempo Win Coin Toss, Elect To Pick Sixth In First Round Of WNBA Draft — The Toronto Tempo won the coin toss against the Portland Fire on Friday and elected to pick sixth in the upcoming WNBA draft. The Fire will have the first pick in the expansion draft, along with the seventh pick in the draft. The Tempo will have the No. 6, 22, and 36 picks in the draft. The Fire will pick No. 7, 21, and 37. items.headline: Naz Reid Fined $50K For Questioning Integrity Of Officials
items.blurb: Naz Reid Fined $50K For Questioning Integrity Of Officials — The NBA fined Naz Reid $50,000 "for questioning the integrity of game officials" during the Minnesota Timberwolves' 110-108 win over the Houston Rockets on Wednesday. Reid received a technical foul and was ejected from the game with 4:13 left in overtime. Reid was ejected for making "a statement that questioned the integrity of the crew,

items.blurb: LeBron passes to Bronny for NBA’s first father-son assist, league says

items.headline: Big update emerges about James Harden's future
items.blurb: Big update emerges about James Harden's future — James Harden’s honeymoon period with the Cleveland Cavaliers still appears to be going strong.

items.headline: Raptors bring the energy in important win over Pelicans
items.blurb: Raptors bring the energy in important win over Pelicans — After a convincing victory against the Pelicans, the Raptors have won their first most important game of their last 10. But Michael Grange explains why Friday’s win carried extra weight.

items.headline: Kawhi Leonard's clutch shot saves Clippers from embarrassing loss
items.blurb: Kawhi Leonard's clutch shot saves Clippers from embarrassing loss — Kawhi Leonard declared his Los Angeles Clippers are "not scared of nobody" going into the playoffs. Friday, his clutch shot saved the Clippers when the 16-win Indiana Pacers put a scare into them.

items.headline: Jokic has triple-double, Murray, Johnson hit late threes to rally Nuggets by Jazz
items.blurb: Jokic has triple-double, Murray, Johnson hit late threes to rally Nuggets by Jazz

items.headline: Nuggets’ Jokic beats third-quarter buzzer with 39-foot bomb
items.blurb: Nuggets’ Jokic beats third-quarter buzzer with 39-foot bomb — Watch as Denver Nuggets star Nikola Jokic throws up a spectacular heave against the Utah Jazz, draining a 39-foot three that beats the buzzer to cut into the Utah Jazz lead heading into the fourth.

items.headline: How Barnes, Walter helped Raptors pick up win over Pelicans
items.blurb: How Barnes, Walter helped Raptors pick up win over Pelicans — Brad Fay, Sherman Hamilton, and Matt Bonner break down the Toronto Raptors’ win over the New Orleans Pelicans and explain how Scottie Barnes and Ja’Kobe Walter helped bring home the victory.

items.headline: ‘No games off’: Barnes on Raptors’ playoff push
items.blurb: ‘No games off’: Barnes on Raptors’ playoff push — Toronto Raptors point guard Scottie Barnes joins Danielle Michaud to discuss the team’s win over the New Orleans Pelicans and their continued playoff push.

items.headline: NBA seeking more punitive anti-tanking penalties, including fining teams 'millions of dollars'
items.blurb: NBA seeking more punitive anti-tanking penalties, including fining teams 'millions of dollars' — In addition to planning to reform the draft lottery in an effort to deter teams from tanking, the NBA is also interested in expanding its ability to penalize teams who manipulate player availability and rotations in an effort to lose.

items.headline: Leonard rises from the elbow and drains game-winner to beat Pacers
items.blurb: Leonard rises from the elbow and drains game-winner to beat Pacers — Los Angeles Clippers forward Kawhi Leonard gets to his spot and elevates over the Indiana Pacers’ Aaron Nesmith and Jay Huff to knock down the game-winning jumper to give the Clippers a 114-113 road victory.

summary: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

Nuclear option should get serious consideration from NBA to combat tanking — Try as the NBA might, but tanking isn't going away. While pondering how to de-incentivize losing, commissioner Adam Silver must consider the nuclear option.

NBA Highlights: Suns 134, Jazz 109 — Jalen Green scored a game-high 31 points, Devin Booker dropped 26 points while adding eight assists, and Grayson Allen picked up 19 points off the bench as the Phoenix Suns defeated the Utah Jazz 134-109.

Paul George plays hero for 76ers in second game after suspension — In only his second game since serving a 25-game suspension for violating the NBA's anti-drug policy, Paul George played the role of hero for the Philadelphia 76ers in their win over the Charlotte Hornets on Saturday night.

NBA Roundup: Pistons strengthen hold on East’s top seed — Tobias Harris led a balanced scoring attack with 18 points on Saturday as the Detroit Pistons strengthened their hold on the top seed in the Eastern Conference with a 109-87 victory over the injury-riddled Minnesota Timberwolves.

NBA Highlights: Grizzlies 125, Bulls 124 — Cedric Coward led the Memphis Grizzlies with 24 points and Tyler Burton added 18 points off the bench as the Grizzlies held on for a 125-124 win over the Chicago Bulls.
NBA Highlights: Hawks 123, Kings 113 — Nickeil Alexander-Walker led the way with 27 points and Jalen Johnson added 26 points and 10 assists as the Atlanta Hawks defeated the Sacramento Kings 123-113.

NBA Highlights: 76ers 118, Hornets 114 — Joel Embiid had a team-high 29 points and Paul George added a 26-point, 13-rebound double-double to help the Philadelphia 76ers beat the Charlotte Hornets 118-114.
NBA Highlights: Pistons 109, Timberwolves 87 — Tobias Harris had 18 points and Jalen Duran had 10 points and 13 rebounds as the Detroit Pistons topped the Minnesota Timberwolves 109-87.
